# Implementation Plan: CareerAI — Resume Analysis Platform

**Branch**: `001-career-ai-core` | **Date**: 2026-06-01 | **Spec**: `.specify/templates/spec-template.md`

**Input**: Feature specification from `.specify/templates/spec-template.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command based on the feature specification and constitution check.

## Summary

Build a web platform where candidates upload a PDF resume and job description, receive an AI-powered compatibility analysis (score 0–100, strengths, weaknesses, suggestions) delivered by the Groq API (Llama 3), with results persisted to SQLite. Architecture uses a strict three-layer separation (Presentation → Service → Data) enforced by the constitution.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Python 3.10+

**Primary Dependencies**: FastAPI 0.100+, Flask 3.0+, pdfplumber 0.10+, Groq SDK (latest), Pydantic v2, httpx 0.27+

**Storage**: SQLite via Python stdlib `sqlite3`

**Testing**: pytest + unittest.mock (Groq API always mocked)

**Target Platform**: Linux container on Render.com free tier / local via Docker Compose

**Project Type**: Web application (Flask frontend + FastAPI backend as separate services)

**Performance Goals**: < 5s end-to-end for PDFs ≤ 10 MB (RNF01)

**Constraints**: < 10 MB upload, 50–10.000 chars job description, Render free tier cold start ~50s acceptable

**Scale/Scope**: MVP for single user at a time, no authentication, no concurrent batch processing

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

| Principle | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| I. Layered Architecture | ✅ PASS | `presentation/`, `service/`, `data/` strictly separated |
| II. Type Safety | ✅ PASS | All functions have complete type hints |
| III. External Integration Isolation | ✅ PASS | `GroqService` and `PDFService` are sole access points |
| IV. Security-First Uploads | ✅ PASS | uuid4 rename + finally-block delete implemented |
| V. Test Coverage Gate | ✅ PASS | `test_input_validator.py`, `test_pdf_service.py`, `test_analysis_service.py` implemented |
| VI. Logging Over Printing | ✅ PASS | `logging` module used throughout; no `print()` in production code |
| VII. Stateless Design | ✅ PASS | No session state; files deleted after each request |

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
.specify/
├── CareerAI.specification.md    # Full project specification (input to /speckit.specify)
├── memory/constitution.md       # Active constitution (output of /speckit.constitution)
└── templates/
    ├── constitution-template.md  # Output of /speckit.constitution
    ├── spec-template.md          # Output of /speckit.specify
    ├── plan-template.md          # This file (output of /speckit.plan)
    ├── tasks-template.md         # Output of /speckit.tasks
    └── checklist-template.md     # Output of /speckit.checklist

docs/
├── constitution.md               # Human-readable constitution (copy for GitHub visibility)
├── schema.md                     # Data model and API contracts (derived from spec RFs)
└── plan.md                       # Implementation plan (derived from RNFs + schema)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
my-project/
│
├── backend/                          # FastAPI service (:8000)
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── presentation/
│   │   │   ├── routes.py             # POST /api/v1/analyze, GET /api/v1/health
│   │   │   └── schemas.py            # AnalysisResponse (Pydantic)
│   │   ├── service/
│   │   │   ├── groq_service.py       # Groq API integration (Llama 3)
│   │   │   ├── pdf_service.py        # PDF text extraction (pdfplumber)
│   │   │   ├── input_validator.py    # Text validation and sanitization
│   │   │   └── analysis_service.py   # JSON response parsing
│   │   ├── data/
│   │   │   └── database.py           # SQLite CRUD operations
│   │   ├── config.py                 # Env var loading (GROQ_API_KEY, etc.)
│   │   └── main.py                   # FastAPI app factory
│   ├── tests/
│   │   ├── test_input_validator.py
│   │   ├── test_pdf_service.py
│   │   ├── test_analysis_service.py
│   │   └── conftest.py
│   ├── requirements.txt
│   └── .env.example
│
├── frontend/                         # Flask service (:5000)
│   ├── templates/
│   │   ├── index.html                # Upload form (US1 entry point)
│   │   └── results.html              # Analysis results display
│   └── static/
│       ├── css/style.css
│       └── js/main.js
│
├── app.py                            # Flask app entry point
├── requirements-frontend.txt
├── Dockerfile
├── docker-compose.yml
└── render.yaml                       # Render.com Blueprint for free-tier deploy
```

**Structure Decision**: Web application (Option 2) with separate `backend/` (FastAPI) and `frontend/` (Flask) services. Flask acts as a BFF (Backend for Frontend) proxying requests to FastAPI via httpx.

## Complexity Tracking

> No constitution violations found. All complexity is justified by the feature requirements.

| Decision | Why Needed | Simpler Alternative Rejected Because |
|---|---|---|
| Two separate services (Flask + FastAPI) | Separation of concerns: Flask handles HTML rendering, FastAPI handles AI/PDF logic | Single Flask app would mix template rendering with AI integration, violating layered architecture |
| Groq API instead of local Ollama | Cloud deployment requires external LLM — Ollama needs GPU/RAM unavailable on Render free tier | Local Ollama cannot be deployed on free-tier cloud infrastructure |
